content: When you send a message online, whether it's an email, a chat, or a text, you want to make sure only the intended recipient can read it. Encryption is a way of scrambling your message so that only the person with the right "key" can unscramble it and read it. Think of it like sending a secret letter in a locked box, where only the person with the key to the box can open it and read the letter. This process happens automatically, without you even realizing it, and is an essential part of online communication to keep your messages private. From personal emails and chats to online banking and shopping, encryption is used to protect all kinds of sensitive information. By using secure messaging apps and keeping your device and browser up-to-date, you can help ensure your messages are encrypted and your online privacy is protected.
